title: The Agency Efficiency Score
subtitle: How Much Profit Are You Leaving on the Table?

persona_selector.text: Which best describes your agency right now?

answers.label: Boutique shop, 10-20 people, founder still in most client work
Growing agency, 20-40 people, building out ops and delivery layers
Established agency, 40-75 people, multiple departments and service lines
Specialized agency with a narrow ICP and repeatable delivery

questions.text: How are inbound leads qualified before they reach a closer?

answers.label: A human manually reviews and calls every lead
Basic form routing, then a team member reviews manually
Form plus a scoring spreadsheet a human updates
Automated scoring with human review of borderline cases
Fully automated qualification with AI scoring and routing

questions.text: How long does it take to respond to a qualified inbound lead?

answers.label: More than 24 hours, sometimes days
Same business day if someone is available
Within 4 hours during working hours
Within 15 minutes during working hours
Under 5 minutes, 24/7, automated

questions.text: How is your sales pipeline tracked and updated?

answers.label: Memory, email threads, and scattered notes
A spreadsheet someone updates weekly
A CRM that team members update manually when they remember
A CRM with some automated activity logging
CRM fully synced with email, calls, and proposals automatically

questions.text: How are proposals and pricing documents created?

answers.label: Custom-written from scratch every time
Copy-paste from an old proposal and edit heavily
Template doc filled in manually per client
Semi-automated generator with human review
AI-generated from intake data with one-click edits

questions.text: How do you track where leads drop off in your pipeline?

answers.label: We don't track this at all
We look at it when revenue dips
Monthly manual review of CRM stages
Weekly dashboards with conversion rates per stage
Real-time dashboards with automated alerts on stage decay

low.headline: Your sales pipeline is costing you deals you never knew you had
low.text: Right now, leads are falling through gaps because qualification, follow-up, and tracking depend on humans remembering to do things. Speed to lead alone is worth a measurable bump in close rate, and you are leaving that on the table every single day.
steps: Install a CRM this week and move every active lead into it, even if the data is messy
Set up an automated instant-response system so no qualified lead waits more than 5 minutes
Build a simple lead scoring rubric and route only qualified leads to a human closer

mid.headline: Your pipeline works, but it is quietly inefficient
mid.text: You have structure, but too much of it runs on manual updates and human memory. The drop-off is not in closing, it is in the dozens of small tasks between stages that nobody owns cleanly.
steps: Automate CRM activity logging so reps stop updating stages by hand
Introduce AI-assisted proposal generation tied to your intake form
Add weekly conversion dashboards so you can see stage decay before it becomes a revenue problem

high.headline: Your pipeline is a genuine asset, now make it compound
high.text: You are already ahead of most agencies on sales ops. The next level is using your pipeline data to train sharper qualification, better forecasting, and AI that drafts the first version of every sales artifact.
steps: Feed closed-won and closed-lost data into an AI scoring model that improves over time
Automate proposal personalization using past deal data and intake answers
Layer in predictive forecasting so you can spot revenue gaps 60 days out

questions.text: How are new client intake materials collected?

answers.label: Back-and-forth emails until we have what we need
A single intake doc the client fills in inconsistently
Structured intake form with manual follow-up
Intake form plus automated reminders for missing items
Smart intake with conditional logic and auto-populated assets

questions.text: How long does it take from contract signed to kickoff call?

answers.label: More than 2 weeks, often longer
Around 10 business days
Within 1 week
Within 3 business days
Within 24 hours, fully automated scheduling

questions.text: How are brand and strategy documents built for new clients?

answers.label: Strategist writes every doc from scratch
We reuse old docs and heavily edit
Templates filled in manually after intake
AI-assisted first drafts reviewed by strategist
Fully AI-generated drafts from intake with light human polish

questions.text: How is project workspace setup handled in your PM tool?

answers.label: Someone manually creates every list, task, and folder
We copy from a template and tweak
Template workspace with a setup checklist
One-click template cloning with a few custom fields
Fully automated workspace provisioning from signed contract

questions.text: How are access, logins, and shared assets collected from clients?

answers.label: Chased over email for weeks
Tracked in a shared doc with manual follow-up
Checklist in PM tool with human reminders
Automated request flow with reminders and status tracking
Self-serve portal with automated ingestion into our systems

low.headline: Your onboarding is burning billable hours and client goodwill
low.text: Every day between contract signed and work started is a day the client questions their decision. Manual intake, scattered docs, and chased access are eating your margin and delaying revenue recognition.
steps: Standardize a single intake form covering every asset, login, and context item you actually need
Build a kickoff checklist and automate reminders for missing client deliverables
Create template workspaces in your PM tool that can be cloned in one click per new client

mid.headline: Onboarding works, but it still leans on one or two heroes
mid.text: You have templates and process, but the system depends on someone remembering to run it. When that person is busy, clients wait, and the first impression of your agency quietly suffers.
steps: Automate workspace provisioning the moment a contract is signed
Use AI to draft brand and strategy docs from your intake form before the kickoff call
Move client access collection to a self-serve portal with automated follow-up

high.headline: Your onboarding is a competitive weapon, sharpen it
high.text: You already onboard faster than most agencies. The upside now is compounding: every onboarding should make the next one faster and generate cleaner data for delivery and reporting.
steps: Feed intake data directly into AI that drafts strategy, creative briefs, and kickoff decks
Auto-provision PM workspaces, shared drives, and reporting dashboards from one trigger
Track onboarding cycle time as a core KPI and optimize the slowest step every quarter

questions.text: How are creative briefs written?

answers.label: From scratch every time by a strategist
Copy-paste from a past brief, edit heavily
Template brief filled in manually
AI-assisted draft from intake and strategy data
Auto-generated briefs tied to client brand and goals

questions.text: How does your team produce first drafts of content?

answers.label: 100% human-written, no AI in the pipeline
Occasional AI use by individual team members
AI used for ideation but not drafting
AI drafts reviewed and edited by specialists
AI drafts tuned on client voice, human polish only

questions.text: How are creative assets versioned and approved internally?

answers.label: Email threads and random file names
Shared drive with manual version labels
PM tool with approval steps people follow sometimes
Structured approval flow with status automation
Fully automated review routing with SLA tracking

questions.text: How repeatable is your production process across clients?

answers.label: Every client gets a custom process
We have loose playbooks nobody follows closely
Documented playbooks per service line
Playbooks plus templates used on every project
Productized delivery with standardized inputs and outputs

questions.text: How do you track time and capacity across production?

answers.label: We don't, it's all in people's heads
Manual time entries nobody fills in consistently
Time tracking tool with spotty adoption
Tracked time tied to projects with weekly reviews
Real-time capacity dashboards with automated alerts

low.headline: Your production engine is the biggest drain on margin you have
low.text: Custom processes and manual drafting mean every project reinvents the wheel. Your best people are spending hours on work that should be templated, automated, or AI-assisted, and it shows up as thin margins and burned-out senior staff.
steps: Document your top 3 services as step-by-step playbooks this month
Introduce AI drafting for at least one deliverable type, starting with written assets
Mandate time tracking on every project so you can see where hours actually go

mid.headline: You have structure, but you are not yet compounding
mid.text: Your playbooks and templates exist, but they are not wired into automation or AI. Each project still requires too much senior attention for work that should be handled by systems.
steps: Move AI from ideation into actual drafting for briefs, captions, and outlines
Standardize review and approval flows inside your PM tool with automated status updates
Track capacity in real time so you can price and staff projects with actual data

high.headline: Your production is strong, now productize and scale it
high.text: You run a tight shop. The next unlock is turning your delivery into a near-productized engine where AI handles first drafts, humans handle judgment, and margins expand with every new client instead of shrinking.
steps: Build AI models tuned on each client's voice to cut drafting time on recurring deliverables
Turn your best service lines into packaged offerings with fixed scope and automated kickoff
Use capacity data to forecast hiring and pricing 90 days out, not reactively

questions.text: How are client status updates delivered?

answers.label: Whenever the account lead remembers
Ad hoc emails when something changes
Weekly manual update written by the account lead
Scheduled updates with templated structure
Automated updates pulled from PM and analytics tools

questions.text: How are client questions routed and answered?

answers.label: Whoever sees the email first tries to answer
Account lead handles everything personally
Shared inbox with manual triage
Shared inbox with routing rules and SLAs
AI-assisted triage with draft responses for the team

questions.text: How are performance reports generated for clients?

answers.label: Built from scratch in slides each month
Copy-paste screenshots into a template
Template report filled in manually from dashboards
Auto-populated report with manual narrative
Fully automated reports with AI-generated insights

questions.text: How do you handle scope creep and out-of-scope requests?

answers.label: We usually just do it to keep the client happy
We push back sometimes, inconsistently
Documented scope but enforcement is manual and slow
Clear scope with a documented change-order process
Automated scope tracking that flags overages in real time

questions.text: How quickly can a client get an answer on project status?

answers.label: They email and wait, sometimes days
Next business day if we are not slammed
Within a few hours during working hours
On-demand dashboard they can check anytime
Real-time client portal with AI assistant for questions

low.headline: Communication gaps are quietly killing your retention
low.text: Clients churn when they feel in the dark, even when the work is good. Right now, your team is either over-communicating in a way that eats hours, or under-communicating in a way that costs renewals.
steps: Standardize a weekly client update format and put it on a calendar
Create a shared client inbox with clear ownership and SLAs
Build a simple monthly reporting template that anyone on the team can fill in

mid.headline: Your communication is reliable, but it is expensive
mid.text: Your team is doing the right things, but too much of it is done by hand. The account leads who should be driving strategy are instead writing updates and assembling reports.
steps: Automate the data-pull portion of client reports so narrative is the only human step
Introduce scope tracking and change-order automation to protect margin
Use AI to draft first-pass responses to routine client questions

high.headline: Your client experience is a retention weapon, push it further
high.text: Clients already feel informed and in control with you. The next step is making that experience scale without your senior team being in every conversation.
steps: Launch a real-time client portal with project status, deliverables, and analytics
Use AI to generate insight summaries on every report, not just the data
Offer a client-facing AI assistant that can answer status and scope questions 24/7

questions.text: How is invoicing handled?

answers.label: Manual invoices created in a doc or PDF tool
Accounting software but invoices created by hand
Accounting software with saved templates
Automated recurring invoices with some manual one-offs
Fully automated billing tied to project milestones

questions.text: How are team meetings and internal comms structured?

answers.label: Constant meetings, no clear agendas
Standing meetings, inconsistent value
Weekly cadences with agendas
Async-first with focused sync meetings
Async-first with AI-generated recaps and action items

questions.text: How is internal knowledge stored and shared?

answers.label: In individual heads and Slack history
Scattered docs across multiple tools
A wiki that is partially updated
Maintained wiki with clear ownership
Searchable knowledge base with AI retrieval

questions.text: How do you handle repetitive admin tasks like scheduling, data entry, and follow-ups?

answers.label: Humans do all of it
A few shortcuts and saved templates
Some Zapier or basic automations in place
Most repetitive tasks automated
AI agents handling admin workflows end to end

questions.text: How clearly do you know your real profit per client and per service?

answers.label: We go on gut feel
We look at revenue but not true cost
Rough margin calculations once a quarter
Monthly profit-per-client tracking
Real-time profitability dashboards per client and service

low.headline: Your back office is quietly taxing every deliverable
low.text: Admin work looks small task by task, but it compounds into hundreds of hours a month. Right now, your team is paying a hidden tax on every project because the operational layer is almost entirely human.
steps: List every repetitive admin task your team does weekly and rank by hours spent
Automate the top 3 with simple tools like Zapier, n8n, or built-in CRM features
Centralize internal knowledge in one searchable place, even if it is just a basic wiki

mid.headline: Your ops are solid, but AI is the missing layer
mid.text: You have systems, but they are mostly glue between tools, not intelligence on top of them. The gap between mid and high is where AI starts handling judgment-light work your humans still touch.
steps: Introduce AI agents for scheduling, follow-ups, and routine data entry
Add AI-generated meeting recaps and action items to every internal sync
Build profit-per-client dashboards so you can see margin in real time, not quarterly

high.headline: Your ops are a genuine moat, now compound the advantage
high.text: You are operating at a level most agencies cannot match. The next move is using your data and automation maturity to build services and pricing models that competitors structurally cannot replicate.
steps: Deploy AI agents that own entire admin workflows end to end, not just steps
Use real-time profitability data to reshape pricing and kill unprofitable service lines
Package your internal automation expertise into a premium offering for your clients

ctas.headline: Want a personalized Loom breakdown of your operation?
ctas.description: Drop your email + I'll review your exact answers, stack them against agencies your size, and send a 5-minute Loom within 48h with the ONE margin leak I'd close first. Async, no scheduling pain, no pitch. If we both see signal, we book a working call from there.

ctas.headline: Founder-led boutique? Automation payback is slower at your scale.
ctas.description: At 10-20 people you're best served by fixing 2-3 high-leverage workflows yourself. Follow me on LinkedIn for tactical breakdowns — when you cross 25 people, retake this and the CTA will change.

ctas.headline: You're running tight — here's what agencies your size miss next.
ctas.description: At your score, the obvious leaks are closed. The next margin comes from capacity-planning telemetry, deliverable-to-invoice cycle automation, and client-side onboarding SLAs. I'm writing teardowns on each — follow along.

ctas.headline: Get the weekly agency ops teardown
ctas.description: One tactical case study + one n8n workflow breakdown per week. How mid-market agencies run lean — no fluff, no pitch.
